architecture.summary: FlowPipe formulates data preparation pipeline synthesis as conditional probabilistic flow generation over a directed acyclic graph. The system uses Conditional Generative Flow Networks (C-GFlowNets) with a Trajectory Balance objective to learn a policy that generates valid operator sequences. Dataset context is injected via Deep Semantic Modulation through Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM), where an LLM derives logical priors that condition the policy's internal activations. A failure-awareness mechanism is incorporated into the flow objective to avoid invalid states and focus search on high-potential regions. Information flows from dataset features through LLM-derived semantic priors into FiLM modulation of policy activations, then through GFlowNet trajectory sampling with trajectory balance rewards.
trained_components: C-GFlowNet policy with trajectory balance objective
FiLM modulation projector mapping LLM priors to policy activations

biblio.title: FlowPipe: LLM-Enhanced Conditional Generative Flow Networks for Data Preparation Pipeline Construction

claims.dataset: two benchmark suites with 74 real-world datasets
claims.statement: FlowPipe outperforms SOTA baselines, improving accuracy by 11.96% on average

claims.dataset: two benchmark suites with 74 real-world datasets

claims.statement: Existing state-of-the-art Multi-DQN methods face three key limitations: decoupled value estimators weaken long-horizon credit assignment, dataset context is only weakly injected into the policy, and exploration is inefficient in a sparse search space with many invalid states
Trajectory Balance objective connects terminal validation rewards with early pipeline decisions
Deep Semantic Modulation through Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM) allows LLM-derived logical priors to condition the policy's internal activations according to dataset semantics
Failure awareness is incorporated into the flow objective to avoid invalid states and concentrate search on high-potential regions
